parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs inhibit cyclo-oxygenase.'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"topic":"Primary Frca Sample Sbas","exam":"primary","curriculum_node_code":null,"model":"openai:gpt-5.5","generated_at":"2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172","questions":[{"stem":"A patient with asthma develops severe bronchospasm after taking over-the-counter diclofenac.","lead":"Increased production of which mediator is most likely to be responsible?","options":[{"text":"Arachidonic acid","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Arachidonic acid is the substrate for both cyclo-oxygenase and lipoxygenase pathways, but it is not the final mediator most responsible for NSAID-induced bronchospasm."},{"text":"Leukotrienes","is_correct":true,"explanation":"Cyclo-oxygenase inhibition diverts arachidonic acid metabolism towards leukotriene production, which can cause bronchoconstriction in susceptible asthmatic patients."},{"text":"Phospholipase A2","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Phospholipase A2 releases arachidonic acid from membrane phospholipids, but NSAID-exacerbated asthma is mainly due to increased leukotrienes."},{"text":"Prostacyclin","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Diclofenac inhibits prostaglandin and prostacyclin synthesis rather than increasing it."},{"text":"Thromboxane A2","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Thromboxane A2 synthesis is reduced by cyclo-oxygenase inhibition and is not the main cause of NSAID-induced bronchospasm."}],"explanation":"Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs inhibit cyclo-oxygenase. In susceptible patients, arachidonic acid metabolism is shunted towards the lipoxygenase pathway, increasing cysteinyl leukotrienes and causing bronchospasm.","curriculum_node_codes":[],"curriculum_node_scores":[]}]}
